name: Yinglong

distinctive_features: wings attached to a serpentine dragon body
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: elongated body with wings spread out

domain_specific_visual.horns: may have small horns on the head

domain_specific_functional.mythological_role: helper of legendary figures like King Yu

relationships.description: Yinglong killed Chiyou, a rebellious deity against the Yellow Emperor.
Yinglong killed Kua Fu, who caused droughts by drinking rivers.
Yinglong helped King Yu control the Great Deluge by guiding canal construction.

contextual_attributes.historical_period: Warring States period to Han dynasty
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: symbol of rain and water control, associated with legendary rulers

contrastive_constraints: NOT a generic dragon without wings
NOT a Western-style dragon with bat-like wings
NOT a purely decorative or ornamental dragon without functional attributes
